title: Bridge Essay: Impairment of perceptual metacognitive accuracy and reduced prefrontal grey matt

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.

**Impairment of perceptual metacognitive accuracy and reduced prefrontal grey matter volume in first-episode psychosis.**. Cogn Neuropsychiatry.. PMID: 29485348. DOI: 10.1080/13546805.2018.1444597. INTRODUCTION: Metacognition, or "thinking about thinking", is ahigher-order thought process that allows for the evaluation of perceptual processes for accuracy. Metacognitive accuracy is associated with the grey matter volume (GMV) in the prefrontal cortex (PFC), an area also impacted in schizophrenia. The present study set out to investigate whether deficits in metacognitive accuracy are present in the early stages of psychosis. METHODS: Metacognitive accuracy in first-episode psychosis (FEP)
